name: Du Quoin State Bank
description_short: Du Quoin State Bank is a community-focused FDIC-insured bank chartered in 1915, operating five locations across Southern Illinois with over $150M in assets.
description_long:
Du Quoin State Bank was chartered and opened for business on April 7, 1915, with $50,000 in capital. The bank's main branch remains in its original downtown Du Quoin building, which has served as a community landmark for over a century. The institution has grown substantially over the decades and now operates five total locations across Southern Illinois, holding more than $150 million in assets. Recent expansion includes new full-service branches in De Soto (opened April 2022) and Sesser (opened 2022), demonstrating continued regional growth.

Du Quoin State Bank offers comprehensive personal, commercial, and governmental banking services. Personal customers can access checking accounts, savings accounts, online banking, mobile banking, debit cards, and credit cards. The bank provides consumer loans with a dedicated application process, real estate financing through separate applications, and commercial lending for business customers. Additional services include eStatements for paperless banking, check reordering, and two drive-up facilities located in Tamaroa (opened 1983) and Du Quoin Southtowne (opened 1987).

The bank differentiates itself as a hometown institution emphasizing personal relationships and community values over the impersonal service of larger national banks. Their stated commitment includes superior customer service, competitive pricing, and community reinvestment. Du Quoin State Bank is FDIC-insured and subject to evaluation by the Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation regarding Community Reinvestment Act compliance, demonstrating regulatory oversight and accountability to low-to-moderate income households in their service areas.

As a traditional brick-and-mortar community bank, Du Quoin State Bank is best suited for customers prioritizing local relationships and face-to-face service. However, customers seeking cutting-edge digital banking experiences or specialized financial products may find offerings more limited compared to large national banks. The bank does provide modern conveniences like mobile and online banking, though the website does not detail specific product rates, fees, or competitive comparisons. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Consumer loans with dedicated application process
Drive-up teller service at multiple locations
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. Government protection
Five convenient locations across Southern Illinois with two drive-up facilities
Over $150 million in assets demonstrates financial stability and longevity since 1915
Comprehensive services including personal, commercial, and governmental banking
Modern digital options including mobile banking, eStatements, and online account management
Community-focused institution subject to Community Reinvestment Act evaluation
Personal attention and relationship-based service model distinct from national chain banks
cons: Limited geographic footprint—only five locations in Southern Illinois, not suitable for those requiring broader regional access
Website lacks specific information on rates, fees, minimum balances, or product terms for informed comparison
No details provided about credit card rewards, loan APRs, or savings account yields
Customer service availability and hours not clearly specified on main pages
Limited information about business loan options, SBA products, or commercial lending terms

contact.address: 15 E Main St, Du Quoin, IL 62832

best_for: Southern Illinois residents seeking personalized banking with community reinvestment values
Small business owners and commercial customers needing relationship-based lending
Customers prioritizing local institution loyalty and face-to-face service over digital-first banking
diagnosis: Du Quoin State Bank is best for Southern Illinois residents and business customers who value community relationships, local decision-making, and stable FDIC-insured banking from a 109-year-old institution. The primary caveat is geographic limitation to five Southern Illinois locations and lack of transparent pricing information, requiring customers to contact branches directly for rates, fees, and specific product terms.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 15 E Main St, Du Quoin, IL 62832
